Description: Every entity is a container identified by URN. No metadata layer — only containers referencing containers via wires.
Code loaded as atomic morphisms into semantic folders. Code never stored in state_payload. Binding code to metadata = structural binding loss.
morphism_log is single source of truth. containers and wires tables are materialized caches. State reconstructible by replay.
DB stores THE graph — superposition of all projected graphs. Multiple wires between same (A,B) via different ports. UNIQUE on 4-tuple. Query collapses superposition.
Gap between n²×|P_s|×|P_t| potential edges and k actual edges IS knowledge. D/R ratio per sub-category is first-class optimization metric.
Authenticated user container. Morphisms grant access edges. No special ontological status.
Application administrator. AuthUser with elevated OWNS edges to system containers.
Root administrator. Transitive OWNS over all containers.
Template container. Reusable subgraph pattern. Instantiation = ADD + LINK sequence.
General-purpose container. Default object in C. Payload in state_payload JSONB.
LLM/ML model container. Provider-agnostic URN. Provider config in state_payload.
Tool container. Discovered via graph traversal. Exposed via MCP protocol surface.
UI rendering surface. Functor output: containers × wires → React component tree.
Execution surface. HTTP/WS/MCP endpoint. Tools wire here to become executable.
GPU device, Docker container, thread pool, VM. Schedulable resource container.
WS channel, MCP session, gRPC stream, HTTP endpoint. Routable communication container.
Postgres instance, disk volume, network segment. Persistable infrastructure container.
Vector embeddings, context window, conversation history. Ephemeral or persistent memory container.
Ownership edge. Automatic transitive propagation. Decomposition: LINK(owner, 'owns', target, 'child').
Hydration permission. Declared transitivity via manifest/wire_config. Decomposition: LINK(actor, 'can_hydrate', target, 'hydrate').
Runtime configuration. Sets initial state before activation. Decomposition: MUTATE(surface, config_payload).
State synchronization. Surface pushes current state to container. Decomposition: MUTATE(container, current_state).
Create + wire container. Decomposition: ADD(container) ; LINK(parent, 'owns', container, 'child').
General-purpose wiring. Decomposition: LINK(source, port_s, target, port_t).
Kernel-level state update. Decomposition: MUTATE(container, kernel_state).
Remove wire. Container persists. May create orphan nodes. Decomposition: UNLINK(source, port_s, target, port_t).
Workload allocation. Kernel/admin schedules work to compute resource. Decomposition: LINK(kernel, 'can_schedule', resource, 'workload').
Transport selection. Protocol adapter routes traffic. Decomposition: LINK(adapter, 'can_route', target, 'transport').
Storage routing. Container persists state through infra. Decomposition: LINK(source, 'can_persist', infra, 'storage').
Cross-instance communication. Morphism bundle exchange between mo:os instances. Decomposition: LINK(kernel_a, 'can_federate', kernel_b, 'peer').
Branch parallel exploration. Fork active state graph into GPU VRAM for parallel DAG scoring. Decomposition: LINK(active_state, 'can_fork', compute_gpu, 'vram_branch'). Status: planned.
Import: manifest.yaml → wires. Direction: codebase → graph. Status: active.
Render: containers × wires → React tree. Direction: graph → browser. Status: active.
Embed: state_payload → R^1536. Direction: graph → vector space. Status: active.
GPU pre-analysis: subgraph → compressed DAG. Direction: graph → compressed repr. Status: planned.
Cross-provider: C_provider → C_standard. Direction: provider → standard. Status: planned.
∅ → Container. Create container.
C × C → Wire. Create wire.
C → C. Update container state.
Wire → ∅. Remove wire.
Top-level container. Root of all ownership trees.
Kernel event loop. Event→Route→Dispatch→Transform→Commit. No business logic.
Postgres ground truth. morphism_log (truth), containers/wires (caches), container_embeddings (functor output).
Live hypergraph superposition. Queries collapse by port type selection.
